description_long: This object is a classic hand grenade with a spherical, dark green metallic body and a matte black safety lever attached to the top. The lever is rectangular and features numerous white scratches and scuff marks, indicating wear and handling. A circular metal pull ring is attached to the lever, serving as the safety pin mechanism. The body of the grenade has a subtle sheen, with a faint green highlight suggesting a metallic or painted finish. The overall construction appears robust, with the lever and pin assembly securely mounted atop the sphere. The grenade's design is utilitarian, with no visible markings or labels aside from the surface wear.
description: A dark green, spherical hand grenade with a scratched black safety lever and a metal pull ring on top.
description_view_0: Front view showing the scratched black safety lever and the circular pull ring partially visible on the left side.
description_view_1: Side view highlighting the lever's rectangular shape and the spherical body, with the pull ring mostly hidden.
description_view_2: Rear view displaying the smooth, green sphere and the lever with the pull ring hanging down the right side.
description_view_3: Opposite side view with the pull ring fully visible, hanging from the lever, and the lever's edge extending outward.
attribute_detonation_mechanism: Detonation is likely initiated by pulling the metal ring to release the safety lever, which then triggers the internal fuse.
